title: Top 10 Movies Of Mahesh Babu That Show His Versatility | Filmfare.com
meta_description: Mahesh Babu’s top performances and iconic roles have shaped modern Telugu cinema and earned him a massive fan following. Here are his top 10 must-watch films.

h1: Top 10 Movies Of Mahesh Babu That Show His Versatility

clean_text: Top 10 Movies Of Mahesh Babu That Show His Versatility Mahesh Babu, also known as the Prince of Tollywood, has established a dominant position for himself in the Telugu film industry, thanks to his versatile acting skills and strong screen presence. Throughout his career, he has been part of many blockbuster hits and received critical acclaim for his performances, hence confirming his standing as one of the leading actors in Indian cinema. From gripping action thrillers to touching dramas, Mahesh Babu's filmography consists of a variety of roles that highlight his acting capabilities. Here are 10 best Mahesh Babu films that are a must-watch. These films not only highlight Mahesh Babu's impressive range as an actor but also offer a glimpse into the evolution of Telugu cinema over time. Whether you are a longtime fan or are just discovering his work, these movies show his impact on the industry. Okkadu is one of the biggest films in Mahesh Babu's career, showing his portrayal of a brave Kabaddi player who protects a young woman from a dangerous faction leader. The film's gripping plot, along with thrilling action sequences and emotional depth, got a positive response from both viewers and critics. Mahesh Babu's acting was highly praised, and the film's success made him a star. Okkadu not only achieved remarkable commercial success as one of the highest-grossing Telugu films of its time but also led to many remakes in different languages. In this film, Mahesh Babu plays a professional assassin who, after being wrongly accused of murder, adopts a new identity to avoid the authorities. The film combines elements of action, suspense, and drama, with Mahesh delivering a subtle performance that shows the character's inner struggles. The direction by Mr. Trivikram Srinivas, along with his sharp dialogues, enhances the story, making it a must-watch. Athadu was well-received by both critics and audiences, solidifying Mahesh Babu's status as a versatile actor. Over time, it has achieved a cult status and continues to be one of the most frequently telecasted Telugu films on television. Pokiri featured Mahesh Babu in the role of an undercover police officer, who tries to join the criminal underworld to bring down a criminal organization. The film's authentic portrayal of crime and justice, along with its exciting action sequences, impressed the fans and viewers. Mahesh's performance of the brave and enigmatic protagonist was widely praised, and his on-screen chemistry with Ileana D'Cruz added a romantic angle to the story. Pokiri enjoyed a huge success at the box office, becoming one of the highest-grossing Telugu films of its time. Its popularity led to adaptations in multiple languages, including the Hindi film Wanted, starring Salman Khan. In Khaleja, Mahesh Babu plays a taxi driver who unexpectedly becomes the saviour of a village afflicted by a mysterious illness. The film blends action, comedy, and spirituality in a unique story. Mahesh's performance as the reluctant hero was both charming and captivating. While it initially did not perform well in theatres, Khaleja has since gained a cult following, praised for its depth and narrative. The re-release of the film in theatres further showed its lasting appeal to fans. In Dookudu, Mahesh Babu plays a police officer who juggles his professional responsibilities and personal obligations, particularly towards his ailing father. The film combines action, humour, and family drama, showing Mahesh's versatility. His comic timing, particularly in scenes with the veteran comedian Brahmanandam, was a highlight. Dookudu achieved both critical acclaim and commercial success, collecting over ₹100 crore and receiving multiple awards. The Los Angeles Times even described it as "The biggest hit you've never heard of, highlighting its global popularity. 1: Nenokkadine is a psychological thriller, featuring Mahesh Babu, that explores the mind of a rock musician suffering from schizophrenia. The film's innovative plot and unconventional storytelling approach showed a new approach from the traditional Telugu cinema. Mahesh Babu's intense performance, effectively conveying the character's inner struggles, received praise. Despite initially receiving mixed reviews, the film has eventually gained a cult status for its bold storytelling and technical excellence. It was an example of Mahesh Babu's ability to work in a variety of roles. In the film Srimanthudu, Mahesh Babu plays a wealthy businessman who dedicates himself to the betterment of a village, highlighting themes of responsibility and community development. The film's socially conscious message deeply connected with viewers, motivating many individuals to start village adoption programs in real life. Mahesh's impactful acting enhanced the story, resulting in a film that was both entertaining and thought-provoking. Srimanthudu achieved huge commercial success, collecting over ₹200 crore, and was honoured with multiple awards, including the Filmfare Award for Best Actor - Telugu. Its success further cemented Mahesh Babu's status as a leading actor in Indian cinema. In Bharat Ane Nenu Mahesh Babu plays a young man who unexpectedly assumes the role of Chief Minister of Andhra Pradesh and tries to bring a systemic change. The film explores themes of political accountability and governance, with Mahesh delivering a compelling performance as a reform-minded leader. His performance was both inspiring and relatable, connecting with audiences from various backgrounds. The film achieved commercial success, collecting over ₹200 crore, and received critical praise for its storytelling and performances. It further showed Mahesh Babu's ability to take on roles with depth and social relevance. Mahesh Babu plays an army officer who is sent to Kurnool to inform about an injured soldier. Problems arise when he finds himself in the middle of uncovering a major scam. The film earned around Rs 260 crore at the box office and was Mahesh Babu's highest-grossing film surpassing Bharat Ane Nenu and Maharshi. It also became the second-highest grossing Telugu film of 2020 and the fourth highest grossing Telugu film at the time of its release. The film is about a conflict between Mahi, a financier and MP named Rajendranath, after his daughter Kalaavathi cons Mahi for her gambling debts. It received mostly mixed reviews from critics. The film collected ₹180-230 crores worldwide becoming the second-highest grossing Telugu film of 2022.
